question_text: The Moon orbits the Earth in approximately 28 days. How many times does the Moon orbit Earth in one year (365 days)?
correct_answer: Approximately 13 times (or 13.04 orbits; accept 12-14 as reasonable estimates)
explanation: To find how many orbits occur in one year, divide the number of days in a year by the orbital period: 365 ÷ 28 ≈ 13.04. Therefore, the Moon completes approximately 13 orbits around Earth in one year.
